After Disney spoke out against his vile “Don’t Say Gay” bill, DeSantis _**retaliated**_ by stripping Disney World of its special district status, jeopardizing Floridians' jobs and likely saddling hard-working taxpayers with horrendous costs.
**Gov. DeSantis’s attack on Disney’s freedom of speech is exhibit A in why an expert on autocracy said,** _**“Ron DeSantis is turning Florida into his own mini-autocracy.”**_
---
But here’s the good news: According to a NBC News poll, **_Disney is more popular in Florida than Ron DeSantis._**

This tells me two things: (1) most Floridians support Disney’s right to free speech and (2) most Floridians _don’t_ support Gov. DeSantis’s authoritarian tactics.

We now have an opportunity to show these folks that there’s a better future for Florida, but reaching those voters costs money — and I can tell you that DeSantis has no shortage of it. He’s funded by far-right, billionaire donors, and they’re perfectly willing to shell out whatever it takes to keep their pal Ron in power.

That’s not freedom. That’s tyranny. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: = = = = = After Disney spoke out against his vile “Don’t Say Gay” bill, DeSantis retaliated by stripping Disney World of its special district status, jeopardizing Floridians' jobs and likely saddling hard-working taxpayers with horrendous costs. Gov. DeSantis’s attack on Disney’s freedom of speech is exhibit A in why an expert on autocracy said, “Ron DeSantis is turning Florida into his own mini-autocracy.” But here’s the good news: According to a NBC News poll, Disney is more popular in Florida than Ron DeSantis. This tells me two things: (1) most Floridians support Disney’s right to free speech and (2) most Floridians don’t support Gov. DeSantis’s authoritarian tactics. We now have an opportunity to show these folks that there’s a better future for Florida, but reaching those voters costs money — and I can tell you that DeSantis has no shortage of it. name: Shahid Buttar
subject: The whole is worse than the sum of its parts
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Shahid Buttar for Congress Committee, 1390 Market St
body:
It’s been two years since George Floyd was murdered. What can we learn from this week’s tragic events? | | |
---
[](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953yRkW_Sw2xr1MvIXEt6jooTbMnsVYqhUO8Xwt4ORz78ORT8Pt3SALA3pMGVDypeKbyy2kgsjsCmnmy8k7FgsqB6wuCJ9K2SYervuUQSBWz0OyldsY10qBFURiLYNTI9hmo75NRoX9d0AD8wb_I6qcDhghmimutXoXvHLtQ2KwwtrfU6TUMhkYw-FGzsc38wqGUDqaK2cVEgR0rgLMrT-F7yOlROkOLTAcqWdSFZR8eLnkDqElsm7Xq7XzCAPj-vhbSo2XfKsbMU58pSB_njjWArrJOx2_ts3oxrrgNVVi6cyBg35mepwK8CJOy7FVTV-enCoLcNFluDWpc-hcIo3yCzSiMxUMbhBMZhN46VncX8xw/3md/GV58ES2GSY-0MaxlLN2jAw/h0/mNRzrWkTyinS5_A-sym59-O_GB8H0tJwhWuBNfCzTj0)
| | Dear Peter, Yesterday was the second anniversary of the murder of George Floyd by police in Minneapolis. It was also the day after our nation’s latest mass shooting, in which 19 students and two teachers died during a 40 minute rampage by a gunman while police stood outside the building, working mostly to prevent parents from attempting to rescue their children. [**We’re working hard to end the era of lethal violence, whether by police or by murderers empowered by idiotic laws and policies making guns more widely available than baby formula. Can you join us today to hold the bipartisan establishment accountable for its continuing failures?**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTCxhAMhTRXK4t_BbI_sQ5MI-xi6PYFURnJqd3AcJKScyu_1bGJY1U1qVVBAUqYGhh4Y8Zc8kJzTRDE5xuvUU1NkLKALc5Nyrm_Rlx-p_Mc6w8INddT2rDQQZecyEeHYvy-v_VDfb97mIUkKSOwWDkDTBdVMaxokPm06WBIgJoh85PaZ710QCVTgg4ibpx-zVGnyb2nuoXW_w4t9sVFtlv9-XdnuJFUozU1TO4vfCx7q8l6TblzMxOXmrnzQ_XWhpQ11m0LZJfhEqDlyQq044rMmQp_sbGi7G0t3tEXBgKXPhGThKeuwnEO-J2-uk9u_6giXRJaGdHP3wLHaa3HcIMo4/3md/GV58ES2GSY-0MaxlLN2jAw/h1/ajlTnSGHg23bZJgKuX2Yg8RUB9ROHzvtCiOefciXu_Y) On the anniversary of George Floyd’s death, President Biden issued an executive order addressing policing. While largely symbolic, one useful thing it did was finally create a national database of police misconduct. We’ve called for that since 2018, and are pleased to see the White House finally step up, even if the move is years late and too many lives short. Other reforms that we brought forward include ending Qualified Immunity so that police can be held responsible in court for their abuses of civil rights, abolishing civil asset forfeiture, legalizing cannabis at the federal level, and ending cash bail across the United States. Each of these measures remains vital, and important parts of a vision that amounts to more than the sum of its parts. Ultimately, our country needs to invest in alternatives to predatory policing, particularly social workers better poised to promote public safety and support people and communities in crisis. [**Can you join us today to help continue the advocacy for long overdue justice that Washington continues to ignore?**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTCxhAMhTRXK4t_BbI_sQ5MI-xi6PYFURnJqd3AcJKScyu_1bGJY1U1qVVBAUqYGhh4Y8Zc8kJzTRDE5xuvUU1NlXinNvTacUiHnFT7BJ22RbyibSExtDKWWtOG_mvUpA0ij25kXchxSogO171B7yrWbsJGKJ2h92pp1AAtDUALHRnQlfllIYc2cyO3BAIw4aY20zR-ssL9sg8JJ84TWpkkg22FKG5A6r415oGph4RTso1A6r-Lxw0BNTA1yrv2QB742KzwHSZ0A84OI1jQdMteTVKnGANACCiRfzy4qbV6N7Xo8yoUuVz7aTKOv0a7g2AEdKu10mOdBy69BrVX_Pkb0/3md/GV58ES2GSY-0MaxlLN2jAw/h2/cvwd6CAH25xXBjzosqwhkKsTGboiXbVujMgYSODutag) The mass shooting in Uvalde, TX reveals many reasons why the era of predatory policing must finally end. First among them is the simple fact that police are incapable of protecting public safety, as demonstrated by any number of examples and a consistent historical pattern. That might reflect their primary mission, which has always been to protect property—not people. Local police in Uvalde did nothing to stop the shooting. They stood outside for 40 minutes while a gunman murdered children inside their classroom. Police may have actively gotten one of the students killed by inviting them to call out for help before subduing the assailant, which one child reportedly did—only to be shot. That police department receives 40% of its town’s budget. But the waste of resources is just the tip of an iceberg. [**Can you contribute to our campaign to improve public safety by shifting resources from an industrial slavery complex to solutions better poised to meet the needs of our communities?**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTCxhAMhTRXK4t_BbI_sQ5MI-xi6PYFURnJqd3AcJKScyu_1bGJY1U1qVVBAUqYGhh4Y8Zc8kJzTRDE5xuvUU1NlX03-BR2JYT6omPBFDs5PT5C9VgN9ZAFxZ5ZYHFAxdjpMw4LwQNeFm6kLehEdGSkOoq5YpLABWC_L5kayz816fYMWDOnXlsljhkd-au6zuMvI19YzUfJWMSq7Zp4sUsGsw-rUqazmC3YgfXPoe6SQQYG6vWhoP1ek95JEmWFlCucJgjBbN85jQWkEVsKC_PIBXZToCyPtKEPczFnVyxnaa4IKZwDJ-tXpO8pvHCH_nrd416CmLkfsRYs3C-OHatL4/3md/GV58ES2GSY-0MaxlLN2jAw/h3/KELpzdCwAeT7gkm6GWhYjelpO7HG-5a4SNeHWBVFXfw) Ultimately, it was a Border Patrol SWAT team that killed the gunman in Uvalde. That, too, indicates problems. In a border town populated predominantly by Latino residents, the role played by a DHS immigration enforcement agency in stopping an attack on a school meant that many parents were afraid to go to the school to rescue their children. When families face a faustian choice between armed vigilantes and hostile government agencies with long track records of human rights violations, what does that suggest? Fascism entails many components. Paramilitarized state authorities are one component. Impunity for lethal state violence is another. Vulnerability to acts of vigilante violence are a third. Our country unfortunately reflects many other components of fascism: xenophobia, international belligerence, runaway military spending, mass incarceration and industrialized slavery, and an increasing fusion of state power and private corporate capital. [**Can you join us today to stop fascism in the United States? Many came to recognize it only recently, but I’ve seen this coming for 20 years and done everything conceivably possible to stop it.**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTCxhAMhTRXK4t_BbI_sQ5MI-xi6PYFURnJqd3AcJKScyu_1bGJY1U1qVVBAUqYGhh4Y8Zc8kJzTRDE5xuvUU1NmPN1-qviSnbgOAGzikkjAFy3GGCwdWGs6S9_Cne87CwQgqnW_G8F0dfw-iYwedn_8mvbH70DyZxcdh9mZ35x5XvLqWVZ4hnpHFMd2bvpRI08p7bL4kc7zVWz1w2QXW2NHodYLgKT_WXdEstapY4dOe5pD3m5770ui4ROqc1FV8yXVLv-TJOQ-GmlJc6-DI8ovH_X4wl1w2uZjJuxKPvvCLD6OqrvZJRmTA3pstyJUmkBRO_q59tjipKzUdr4x4xUE/3md/GV58ES2GSY-0MaxlLN2jAw/h4/_PwaOc5bUUW9QScsSIuo54dGTOHn9771Ws19xZbBW9I) After two years grappling with the Covid pandemic, many Americans long for a return to some normal. It’s an understandable wish—but it’s also increasingly impossible as our social fabric continues to fray at the seams. What passed for “normal” before masked deep seated inequities, and a roiling cauldron of cultural conflict irresponsibly stoked by many aspirants to power. **With so many crises and a seemingly endless onslaught of incidents indicating social collapse, it can be easy to grow overwhelmed. But this is no time to hide.** **How we each take action in the present will determine the course of the future.** Thank you for standing with us to defend it from the failures of the past! Your voice,

clean_body: It’s been two years since George Floyd was murdered. What can we learn from this week’s tragic events? | | | | | Dear Peter, Yesterday was the second anniversary of the murder of George Floyd by police in Minneapolis. It was also the day after our nation’s latest mass shooting, in which 19 students and two teachers died during a 40 minute rampage by a gunman while police stood outside the building, working mostly to prevent parents from attempting to rescue their children. On the anniversary of George Floyd’s death, President Biden issued an executive order addressing policing. While largely symbolic, one useful thing it did was finally create a national database of police misconduct. We’ve called for that since 2018, and are pleased to see the White House finally step up, even if the move is years late and too many lives short. Other reforms that we brought forward include ending Qualified Immunity so that police can be held responsible in court for their abuses of civil rights, abolishing civil asset forfeiture, legalizing cannabis at the federal level, and ending cash bail across the United States. Each of these measures remains vital, and important parts of a vision that amounts to more than the sum of its parts. Ultimately, our country needs to invest in alternatives to predatory policing, particularly social workers better poised to promote public safety and support people and communities in crisis. The mass shooting in Uvalde, TX reveals many reasons why the era of predatory policing must finally end. First among them is the simple fact that police are incapable of protecting public safety, as demonstrated by any number of examples and a consistent historical pattern. That might reflect their primary mission, which has always been to protect property—not people. Local police in Uvalde did nothing to stop the shooting. They stood outside for 40 minutes while a gunman murdered children inside their classroom. Police may have actively gotten one of the students killed by inviting them to call out for help before subduing the assailant, which one child reportedly did—only to be shot. That police department receives 40% of its town’s budget. But the waste of resources is just the tip of an iceberg. Ultimately, it was a Border Patrol SWAT team that killed the gunman in Uvalde. That, too, indicates problems. In a border town populated predominantly by Latino residents, the role played by a DHS immigration enforcement agency in stopping an attack on a school meant that many parents were afraid to go to the school to rescue their children. When families face a faustian choice between armed vigilantes and hostile government agencies with long track records of human rights violations, what does that suggest? Fascism entails many components. Paramilitarized state authorities are one component. Impunity for lethal state violence is another. Vulnerability to acts of vigilante violence are a third. Our country unfortunately reflects many other components of fascism: xenophobia, international belligerence, runaway military spending, mass incarceration and industrialized slavery, and an increasing fusion of state power and private corporate capital. After two years grappling with the Covid pandemic, many Americans long for a return to some normal. It’s an understandable wish—but it’s also increasingly impossible as our social fabric continues to fray at the seams. What passed for “normal” before masked deep seated inequities, and a roiling cauldron of cultural conflict irresponsibly stoked by many aspirants to power. With so many crises and a seemingly endless onslaught of incidents indicating social collapse, it can be easy to grow overwhelmed. But this is no time to hide. How we each take action in the present will determine the course of the future. Thank you for standing with us to defend it from the failures of the past! Your voice, ShahidPS -- Want to help, but can't contribute financially? Your time can make a big difference from wherever you live! to end the era of bipartisan corruption in Washington. | ---|---|--- | | | Paid for by Shahid Buttar for Congress Committee, 1390 Market St., Suite 200, San Francisco, CA 94102 | | Sent via . name: Kevin Kiley
subject: Something Fishy Happened
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kevin Kiley for Congress 9458 Treelake Road, Granite Bay, CA 95746, United States
body:
|
---
Hi Peter, I've just published a new blog post: [The Riot Act](https://kevinkiley.nationbuilder.com/r?u=Bjc8XUdeJ9yV6eR3mI9Z6HRd5urWv8u8i-Pa-jQziorBtCnK9IzajtXIzROHVJqr&e=eff202566bfe6143aa18c9c627e100fe&utm_source=kevinkiley&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=may26_final&n=1) Something very fishy happened yesterday. We again forced a vote to suspend the gas tax, beginning the day with a press conference at Stop & Shop Gasoline. You can see my remarks [here](https://kevinkiley.nationbuilder.com/r?u=Bjc8XUdeJ9yV6eR3mI9Z6HRd5urWv8u8i-Pa-jQzior0u11dpbqeDsFI4RhD_VSFffenIxP-qgWuWoY950Jt3w&e=eff202566bfe6143aa18c9c627e100fe&utm_source=kevinkiley&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=may26_final&n=2). When we made the motion at the start of the Assembly Floor Session (which lasted ten hours), the initial vote was 30 Yes, 32 No. On our first attempt in March, not a single Democrat voted yes; this time 10 Democrats did. Our strategy of keeping on the pressure worked. But then, apparently, someone read the defecting Democrats the riot act. It may have been Newsom himself, as shortly after the vote they were with him for a press conference. When they returned, they _changed their votes._ One by one, they marched before the whole Assembly and announced the vote change. (See an example [here](https://kevinkiley.nationbuilder.com/r?u=Bjc8XUdeJ9yV6eR3mI9Z6HRd5urWv8u8i-Pa-jQziorCKIE27go1U3NM22eYKJoAuVGHlCA_ELiG944cE9FwmA&e=eff202566bfe6143aa18c9c627e100fe&utm_source=kevinkiley&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=may26_final&n=3).) By the time this Soviet-style reeducation was complete, only two Democrats remained in favor, and the [final tally](https://kevinkiley.nationbuilder.com/r?u=Bjc8XUdeJ9yV6eR3mI9Z6HRd5urWv8u8i-Pa-jQziop-_GB1R3rLNX6Hw-AU6Bg_dIGTTvZV-sRLeWNOktJh_Q&e=eff202566bfe6143aa18c9c627e100fe&utm_source=kevinkiley&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=may26_final&n=4) was 23-47. That is the way of our Capitol. Even when a Legislator wants to do the right thing, they are bribed or bullied out of it. But this isn’t over: we at least got the 10 to join our caucus in urging the Speaker to bring the bill back by Friday. Meanwhile, I am fighting to stop Newsom’s draconian [new restrictions](https://kevinkiley.nationbuilder.com/r?u=YqgukYA3Mrlri-dqG7I8Trwmtk_uyVQ0nz6QZoJharOG67GdIfJasKrkuFBbrXZdk1Rghi7iWfyvWZtFkZT78g&e=eff202566bfe6143aa18c9c627e100fe&utm_source=kevinkiley&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=may26_final&n=5) on water use: 5-minute showers, no baths, no using a hose for yardwork. There’s absolutely no reason Californians should be forced to change their lifestyles because of political ineptitude. When you’re told to take a shorter shower as water flows abundantly into the ocean, your government is broken. Kevin Kiley
California Legislator ***_12 Days to Election Day_. [Help me get across the finish fine and fight to stop Biden and Pelosi from ruining our country.](https://kevinkiley.nationbuilder.com/r?u=09K6E4GvoPozZ73mzf50y5etLj1_9wCfyRoQ3wvVmv-GOhHPWzgYbh54Gph7Pyuk3g2mp41Qa8XyLKk0CnWqFcPhfo-70bh2d9FGRmWv-G172QGsW76cwQP_qMTr_tK1&e=eff202566bfe6143aa18c9c627e100fe&utm_source=kevinkiley&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=may26_final&n=6)
Paid for by Kevin Kiley for Congress 9458 Treelake Road, Granite Bay, CA 95746, United States
This email was sent to [dpwillis67@gmail.com](dpwillis67@gmail.com). To stop receiving emails, [click here](https://kevinkiley.nationbuilder.com/unsubscribe?e=eff202566bfe6143aa18c9c627e100fe&utm_source=kevinkiley&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=may26_final&n=7).

name: Peter Welch
subject: This is a big moment for our campaign. Can you pitch in?
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY WELCH FOR VERMONT
body:
With the Senate hanging in the balance, we need All Hands On Deck.

# [](https://act.welchforvermont.com/go/144?t=2&akid=453%2E61531%2Ed36KF7)
Hello,
I got in this race for Senate because we’re at a critical moment in our history. With our Democratic majority hanging in the balance, we cannot afford to lose a single seat. That means we have to keep Vermont blue.
Mitch McConnell and national Republicans have already identified Vermont as a target. And with them planning to spend $141 million trying to win back the Senate, hitting our end-of-month fundraising goal couldn't be more important. If we fall short, the GOP will be that much more likely to take this seat and flip the Senate.
Everything—lowering prescription drug prices, gun violence prevention, a Green New Deal, and so much more—is on the line.
[**Can you chip in $5 before Tuesday’s end-of-month deadline? Everything you donate will go directly to keeping Vermont—and the Senate—blue.**](https://act.welchforvermont.com/go/2748?t=3&akid=453%2E61531%2Ed36KF7)
_If you’ve saved payment info with ActBlue Express, your donation will process automatically:_

---

---

---
| [$100](https://act.welchforvermont.com/go/2748?t=11&amount=100&akid=453%2E61531%2Ed36KF7)
---
| [OTHER](https://act.welchforvermont.com/go/2748?t=13&akid=453%2E61531%2Ed36KF7)
---

---
Here’s the thing about the GOP: they’re fighting for failure. They’ve filibustered everything from raising the minimum wage to protecting voting rights. They’re fighting against the will of the people they were elected to serve, and we have to come together to keep them from controlling the Senate.
We’re building a grassroots coalition that will defend our Democratic majority. But we need All Hands On Deck to get it done.
[**Chip in $5 to help build a Vermont and an America that works for all of us.**](https://act.welchforvermont.com/go/2748?t=7&akid=453%2E61531%2Ed36KF7)
Thank you for being in the fight,
Peter

name: Cheri Beasley for Senate
subject: Cheri will be the 51st vote
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Cheri Beasley for North Carolina
body:
Cheri knows Washington is failing our families.
#

We need a champion for our families in the U.S. Senate. For too long, our progress has been blocked because DC insiders like our opponent spend more time listening to corporate special interests and lobbyists than the people they’re meant to serve.
National Republican insiders like Ted Budd are perfectly fine with a broken status quo that keeps our economy and democracy rigged in favor of the giant corporations and the wealthy interests who fund their campaigns.
That’s not okay with Cheri. She knows Washington is failing our families, and she will not hesitate to be the 51st vote to deliver meaningful change.
[**If you’re ready to expand the majority and make real change in the Senate, please consider making a $3 donation to Cheri Beasley today.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cb-em-may22?refcode=em220526-1-ma&refcode2=948.357359.LiS4aw&t=1&akid=948%2E357359%2ELiS4aw)

Your support is critical if we’re going to have to take on such powerful, well-funded interests and flip this seat.

name: George Logan
subject: What happened to being inclusive?
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY GEORGE LOGAN
body:
The CT Democrats posted this tweet, which adapts the cadence of a monkey in the famous children’s book Curious George. |

---
Peter,

The CT Democrats posted this tweet, which adapts the cadence of a monkey in the famous children’s book _Curious George_. **And we all know historically, comparing an African American to a monkey is one of the most racist slanders in the books.**

I have refused to play the race card, but Democrats have taken this to a whole new level. I’m not running for Congress because of my skin color; **I am running because I know I have what it takes to be the best representative for the people of Connecticut.**

I love the people of this district, and I love this state; it’s my home! I have seen with my own eyes the pain and struggle our people are suffering from, and it’s due to the Democrat monopoly in our state.

**I refuse to take part in their games.** I will continue to stay focused on the real issues that are affecting you and every hardworking American that the Left continues to ignore.

Sincerely,

George S. Logan

name: Gary Sinise
subject: These heroes are counting on you
body:
| | | [](http://email.alert.republicantaskforce.com/c/eJyFz8FqwzAMBuCnSW4JsZw49iGHwuitO4zBjkaW5dYsTYLtdq8_dy8w0EFIIH2_Xzx5FNTGBQaAYQIljJxA9m4elQ4kTEA1EE_NOODKqfSJj4dbI-FWMH-HPRH3tN_b2yLRzCSEC6ylGJwyUvHsQM_AhMGM7dfFXjCucbu-452XVIJN2SJbfFKX4xYz26kD1QFY0a7LrZQjN_LUwLlWZnok7nFjv5fXyzqrZ9KL0j25cMItd8Rb7eqKnHfBVVMAFgHMZLR0jTzn_VHJlnbPjXz7-DyDHrRu0-KPn7iuMau5Zr3eK_QvV_nH-QsIdWji)
---
Fellow American, It’s Gary Sinise here. You may know me from my roles in movies like _Apollo 13_ and _The Green Mile_ — but the role that changed my life, and inspired my enduring connection to our defenders, veterans, and first responders, was as Lt. Dan in _Forrest Gump_. **This year, I’m honored to again serve as an Honorary Grand Marshal for the National Memorial Day Parade in Washington, D.C.** The National Memorial Day Parade is an event like no other ... and this year, the American Veterans Center is bringing it BACK in full force, with 5,000+ participants marching down Constitution Avenue before a crowd of 250,000+ in-person, as well as T.V. broadcast that reaches 100 million households around the country. [**Fellow American, the National Memorial Day Parade is PRIMARILY FUNDED by private contributions, including from generous Americans like you. Will you help chip in today to help support the Parade with a gift of $25, $50, $75, $150, or more?**](http://email.alert.republicantaskforce.com/c/eJyFz8FqwzAMBuCnSW4JsZw49iGHwuitO4zBjkaW5dYsTYLtdq8_dy8w0EFIIH2_Xzx5FNTGBQaAYQIljJxA9m4elQ4kTEA1EE_NOODKqfSJj4dbI-FWMH-HPRH3tN_b2yLRzCSEC6ylGJwyUvHsQM_AhMGM7dfFXjCucbu-452XVIJN2SJbfFKX4xYz26kD1QFY0a7LrZQjN_LUwLlWZnok7nFjv5fXyzqrZ9KL0j25cMItd8Rb7eqKnHfBVVMAFgHMZLR0jTzn_VHJlnbPjXz7-DyDHrRu0-KPn7iuMau5Zr3eK_QvV_nH-QsIdWji)
---
[**Support the National Memorial Day Parade**](http://email.alert.republicantaskforce.com/c/eJyFz8FqwzAMBuCnSW4JsZw49iGHwuitO4zBjkaW5dYsTYLtdq8_dy8w0EFIIH2_Xzx5FNTGBQaAYQIljJxA9m4elQ4kTEA1EE_NOODKqfSJj4dbI-FWMH-HPRH3tN_b2yLRzCSEC6ylGJwyUvHsQM_AhMGM7dfFXjCucbu-452XVIJN2SJbfFKX4xYz26kD1QFY0a7LrZQjN_LUwLlWZnok7nFjv5fXyzqrZ9KL0j25cMItd8Rb7eqKnHfBVVMAFgHMZLR0jTzn_VHJlnbPjXz7-DyDHrRu0-KPn7iuMau5Zr3eK_QvV_nH-QsIdWji)
---
[](http://email.alert.republicantaskforce.com/c/eJyFz8FqwzAMBuCnSW4JsZw49iGHwuitO4zBjkaW5dYsTYLtdq8_dy8w0EFIIH2_Xzx5FNTGBQaAYQIljJxA9m4elQ4kTEA1EE_NOODKqfSJj4dbI-FWMH-HPRH3tN_b2yLRzCSEC6ylGJwyUvHsQM_AhMGM7dfFXjCucbu-452XVIJN2SJbfFKX4xYz26kD1QFY0a7LrZQjN_LUwLlWZnok7nFjv5fXyzqrZ9KL0j25cMItd8Rb7eqKnHfBVVMAFgHMZLR0jTzn_VHJlnbPjXz7-DyDHrRu0-KPn7iuMau5Zr3eK_QvV_nH-QsIdWji)
---
When the American Veterans Center first called me more than 15 years ago and invited me to the National Memorial Day Parade, I was honored to join the event to remember our fallen heroes. The American Veterans Center revived the Parade in 2005 after it had been dormant for nearly 70 years. Now, in 2022, they’re bringing the Parade back to its full-scale glory after two years of pandemic precautions. Every American should be proud that generations of our brave servicemen and servicewomen, from the last of our “Greatest Generation” heroes of World War II to our veterans from Afghanistan and Iraq, will get the public thanks for their service that they deserve this year. But I know that the defenders and families of the fallen who will join me in the National Memorial Day Parade will be thinking of their friends and loved ones who made the ultimate sacrifice for our country — the brave Americans we honor on Memorial Day. **Sadly, our country is losing hundreds of World War II veterans each day, so the mission of the National Memorial Day Parade is _more important now than ever_ — as this year’s event may be the LAST opportunity for our WWII heroes to commemorate their friends who never came home.** [**Please make your most generous contribution of $25, $50, $75, $150, $250, $500, to support the National Memorial Day Parade and make it the tribute that our nation’s heroes deserve.**](http://email.alert.republicantaskforce.com/c/eJyFz8FqwzAMBuCnSW4JsZw49iGHwuitO4zBjkaW5dYsTYLtdq8_dy8w0EFIIH2_Xzx5FNTGBQaAYQIljJxA9m4elQ4kTEA1EE_NOODKqfSJj4dbI-FWMH-HPRH3tN_b2yLRzCSEC6ylGJwyUvHsQM_AhMGM7dfFXjCucbu-452XVIJN2SJbfFKX4xYz26kD1QFY0a7LrZQjN_LUwLlWZnok7nFjv5fXyzqrZ9KL0j25cMItd8Rb7eqKnHfBVVMAFgHMZLR0jTzn_VHJlnbPjXz7-DyDHrRu0-KPn7iuMau5Zr3eK_QvV_nH-QsIdWji)
---
[**Support the National Memorial Day Parade**](http://email.alert.republicantaskforce.com/c/eJyFz8FqwzAMBuCnSW4JsZw49iGHwuitO4zBjkaW5dYsTYLtdq8_dy8w0EFIIH2_Xzx5FNTGBQaAYQIljJxA9m4elQ4kTEA1EE_NOODKqfSJj4dbI-FWMH-HPRH3tN_b2yLRzCSEC6ylGJwyUvHsQM_AhMGM7dfFXjCucbu-452XVIJN2SJbfFKX4xYz26kD1QFY0a7LrZQjN_LUwLlWZnok7nFjv5fXyzqrZ9KL0j25cMItd8Rb7eqKnHfBVVMAFgHMZLR0jTzn_VHJlnbPjXz7-DyDHrRu0-KPn7iuMau5Zr3eK_QvV_nH-QsIdWji)
---
The National Memorial Day Parade costs a fraction of other major public events to put on each year. In fact, _the entire parade_ costs about as much as just THREE balloons in the Macy’s Thanksgiving Day Parade or TWO floats in the Rose Bowl Parade! _That means that every dollar that’s contributed by a generous American like you makes a difference for this important event._ Your support of the American Veterans Center not only helps the Parade, but also supports great programs like:
* Record hundreds of oral histories of veterans from World War II to Today before we lose these stories forever.
* Hold our National Youth Summit which allows young students a chance to hear first-hand from veterans from WWII, Korea, and Vietnam.
* Produce veteran focused T.V. documentaries and specials that remind Americans the price of our freedom.

---
[](http://email.alert.republicantaskforce.com/c/eJyFz8FqwzAMBuCnSW4JsZw49iGHwuitO4zBjkaW5dYsTYLtdq8_dy8w0EFIIH2_Xzx5FNTGBQaAYQIljJxA9m4elQ4kTEA1EE_NOODKqfSJj4dbI-FWMH-HPRH3tN_b2yLRzCSEC6ylGJwyUvHsQM_AhMGM7dfFXjCucbu-452XVIJN2SJbfFKX4xYz26kD1QFY0a7LrZQjN_LUwLlWZnok7nFjv5fXyzqrZ9KL0j25cMItd8Rb7eqKnHfBVVMAFgHMZLR0jTzn_VHJlnbPjXz7-DyDHrRu0-KPn7iuMau5Zr3eK_QvV_nH-QsIdWji)
---
[**Please join me in support of the National Memorial Day Parade with a gift of $25, $50, $75, $150, $250, $500, or more right now.**](http://email.alert.republicantaskforce.com/c/eJyFz8FqwzAMBuCnSW4JsZw49iGHwuitO4zBjkaW5dYsTYLtdq8_dy8w0EFIIH2_Xzx5FNTGBQaAYQIljJxA9m4elQ4kTEA1EE_NOODKqfSJj4dbI-FWMH-HPRH3tN_b2yLRzCSEC6ylGJwyUvHsQM_AhMGM7dfFXjCucbu-452XVIJN2SJbfFKX4xYz26kD1QFY0a7LrZQjN_LUwLlWZnok7nFjv5fXyzqrZ9KL0j25cMItd8Rb7eqKnHfBVVMAFgHMZLR0jTzn_VHJlnbPjXz7-DyDHrRu0-KPn7iuMau5Zr3eK_QvV_nH-QsIdWji) But no matter how much you can give to this important cause, I want to ask something additional of you.

On Memorial Day — this year and every year — please join me in honoring those who made the ultimate sacrifice for our freedoms. Come to the National Memorial Day Parade in D.C. Visit a local veterans’ cemetery. Send a note to a family of the fallen. Or simply take a quiet moment to think about the men and women who gave their lives to defend OUR country. _I believe we can never do enough to express our gratitude to our defenders, veterans, and first responders, but we can always do a little more._ Fellow American, thank you for your support of America's heroes, and please — [**if you're able, make a gift to help fund the National Memorial Day Parade right now.**](http://email.alert.republicantaskforce.com/c/eJyFz8FqwzAMBuCnSW4JsZw49iGHwuitO4zBjkaW5dYsTYLtdq8_dy8w0EFIIH2_Xzx5FNTGBQaAYQIljJxA9m4elQ4kTEA1EE_NOODKqfSJj4dbI-FWMH-HPRH3tN_b2yLRzCSEC6ylGJwyUvHsQM_AhMGM7dfFXjCucbu-452XVIJN2SJbfFKX4xYz26kD1QFY0a7LrZQjN_LUwLlWZnok7nFjv5fXyzqrZ9KL0j25cMItd8Rb7eqKnHfBVVMAFgHMZLR0jTzn_VHJlnbPjXz7-DyDHrRu0-KPn7iuMau5Zr3eK_QvV_nH-QsIdWji)

Sincerely,

Gary Sinise
Honorary Grand Marshal
National Memorial Day Parade

clean_body: | | | Fellow American, It’s Gary Sinise here. You may know me from my roles in movies like Apollo 13 and The Green Mile — but the role that changed my life, and inspired my enduring connection to our defenders, veterans, and first responders, was as Lt. Dan in Forrest Gump. This year, I’m honored to again serve as an Honorary Grand Marshal for the National Memorial Day Parade in Washington, D.C. The National Memorial Day Parade is an event like no other ... and this year, the American Veterans Center is bringing it BACK in full force, with 5,000+ participants marching down Constitution Avenue before a crowd of 250,000+ in-person, as well as T.V. broadcast that reaches 100 million households around the country. When the American Veterans Center first called me more than 15 years ago and invited me to the National Memorial Day Parade, I was honored to join the event to remember our fallen heroes. The American Veterans Center revived the Parade in 2005 after it had been dormant for nearly 70 years. Now, in 2022, they’re bringing the Parade back to its full-scale glory after two years of pandemic precautions. Every American should be proud that generations of our brave servicemen and servicewomen, from the last of our “Greatest Generation” heroes of World War II to our veterans from Afghanistan and Iraq, will get the public thanks for their service that they deserve this year. But I know that the defenders and families of the fallen who will join me in the National Memorial Day Parade will be thinking of their friends and loved ones who made the ultimate sacrifice for our country — the brave Americans we honor on Memorial Day. Sadly, our country is losing hundreds of World War II veterans each day, so the mission of the National Memorial Day Parade is more important now than ever — as this year’s event may be the LAST opportunity for our WWII heroes to commemorate their friends who never came home. The National Memorial Day Parade costs a fraction of other major public events to put on each year. In fact, the entire parade costs about as much as just THREE balloons in the Macy’s Thanksgiving Day Parade or TWO floats in the Rose Bowl Parade! That means that every dollar that’s contributed by a generous American like you makes a difference for this important event. Your support of the American Veterans Center not only helps the Parade, but also supports great programs like: Record hundreds of oral histories of veterans from World War II to Today before we lose these stories forever. Hold our National Youth Summit which allows young students a chance to hear first-hand from veterans from WWII, Korea, and Vietnam. * Produce veteran focused T.V. documentaries and specials that remind Americans the price of our freedom. But no matter how much you can give to this important cause, I want to ask something additional of you. On Memorial Day — this year and every year — please join me in honoring those who made the ultimate sacrifice for our freedoms. Come to the National Memorial Day Parade in D.C. Visit a local veterans’ cemetery. Send a note to a family of the fallen. Or simply take a quiet moment to think about the men and women who gave their lives to defend OUR country. I believe we can never do enough to express our gratitude to our defenders, veterans, and first responders, but we can always do a little more. Fellow American, thank you for your support of America's heroes, and please — Sincerely, Gary Sinise Honorary Grand Marshal National Memorial Day Parade | The mission of the American Veterans Center and the World War II Veterans Committee is to preserve and promote the legacy and experiences of America's veterans and active duty service personnel from World War II through today. A non-profit educational organization, the AVC and WWIIVC are funded solely through generous contributions from people like you. body:
A leader is someone that fights for everyone in their district.

| A leader is someone that fights for everyone in their district.

A leader is someone that is a committed fighter for causes that affect the lives of Arizonans on a daily basis.

A leader is honest, ethical, and committed to his district.

A leader is not David Schweikert.

Now more than ever, Arizona needs a leader that understands the issues that Arizonans face and who is ready to wake up every day and fight for their rights in Congress. But I can’t do this without all of you. [_**So, friend, will you please consider donating even $5 to support my campaign and make history by electing the first Black Congressman for Arizona?**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/45779906/347452892/-1304906161?refcode=BLU-HODG-20220526-1-EM&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NzI5NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmZTNhOWYwYi00ZGRkLWVjMTEtYjY1Ni0yODE4NzhiODUxMTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=YgVRPkiD-RX0y43YwyRBgh38Dmq7HQ6O38Z4gWfu2G4=)

To let our voices be heard and win this race, we need the support of a grassroots, people-powered movement. [_**Let's show our opponent that we are a force to be reckoned with by contributing a few dollars today.**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/45779907/347452894/-1304906161?refcode=BLU-HODG-20220526-1-EM&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NzI5NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmZTNhOWYwYi00ZGRkLWVjMTEtYjY1Ni0yODE4NzhiODUxMTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=YgVRPkiD-RX0y43YwyRBgh38Dmq7HQ6O38Z4gWfu2G4=)

Let's make history together,

Jevin

A leader is someone that is a committed fighter for causes that affect the lives of Arizonans on a daily basis. A leader is honest, ethical, and committed to his district. A leader is not David Schweikert. Now more than ever, Arizona needs a leader that understands the issues that Arizonans face and who is ready to wake up every day and fight for their rights in Congress. But I can’t do this without all of you. = With the end-of-month deadline coming up next week, we have to show David Schweikert that we need a new leader that is for the people and not for himself. We can’t keep letting our leaders choose money over our own people in this district. We need a leader that will choose the people every day. To let our voices be heard and win this race, we need the support of a grassroots, people-powered movement. = Let's make history together, Jevin Sent from my iPhone * | | Paid for by Friends of Jevin D. _ Peter,

While most of America will be ringing in Summer with a three-day weekend, here at Team Skarlatos we’re planning to make the most of folks having an extra day off to relax at home/in their backyard.

What will we be doing you might be wondering? **We’ll be door knocking!**

Yep – this time of year in South West Oregon is lovely and we plan to make the most of it. Door knocking isn’t anyone’s favorite thing to do as it pertains to campaign activities, but it gives any candidate great exposure to voters; door knocking gives us the opportunity to reach people we may not otherwise be able to reach through more traditional means.

We’ve signed up 10 volunteers to work with us over the three-day weekend – folks who are doing this because they know what is at stake in the November election – and we’re coming to you today to see if you might be able to help sponsor these volunteers.

Here is what we mean:

name: Laura Nevitt, Keystone Progress
subject: This is our America.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Keystone Progress Education Fund, www
body:
Dear Friend,

I don’t know about you, but I woke up this morning after ANOTHER mass shooting at a school, and I am heartbroken for the families that have been forever changed and torn apart. And I am outraged at our elected officials — mostly Republicans — who refuse to do anything to address gun violence.

When the head coach of an NBA team [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=vPvf5RgCU08] and a late-night talk show host [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=OF_GofUF_y4] show more compassion
and outrage than our elected leaders, we know we have a problem.

More guns are not the answer.
Arming teachers is not the answer.
Single entry doors are not the answer.
Armed guards at school is not the answer — and let’s be clear, there were police and security guards on site in Uvalde.

Keeping guns out of the hands of teenagers and those who should not have them is the answer — we need red flag laws.

Background checks are the answer.
Waiting periods are the answer.
Safe storage laws are the answer.

Nearly 90% of Americans , regardless of party affiliation, think we should pass common sense gun laws, and yet Republicans in Congress and in state legislatures across the country continue to block these much-needed reforms.

Why are the politicians we elect not doing what we, their bosses (their constituents) need them to do? The answer is pretty simple: money.

They are more concerned about donations and holding on to power than doing the right thing. Texas’s Gov. Abbott went to a fundraiser the evening after the second largest mass shooting in his state and lawmakers are flocking to a NRA convention in Houston just days after 19 children and two teachers were murdered by guns 300 miles away.

And, for those who say that this isn’t the time to politicize this, tell me, when is the right time? This is the 212 th mass shooting THIS YEAR! Enough is enough. We need to start holding those who block gun reform efforts accountable.

One way we do this is to VOTE . I know this may not feel like it is enough, but it is a VERY powerful tool, one we each have and must use accordingly. We can also speak out bravely, like former Congressman Beto O’Rourke [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=aS4ik6B61Ro] did when he told Gov. Abbott “This is on you!” at a press conference.

To learn more about gun violence, sensible gun safety legislation, and what you can do to help, check out these resources:

https://giffords.org/
https://www.everytown.org/state/pennsylvania/

Laura Nevitt
Operations Manager
Keystone Progress

https://www.facebook.com/KPEdFund/ [https://www.facebook.com/KPEdFund/]

354 S Atlantic Ave
Pittsburgh, PA 15224

And I am outraged at our elected officials — mostly Republicans — who refuse to do anything to address gun violence. When the head coach of an NBA team [] and a late-night talk show host [] show more compassion and outrage than our elected leaders, we know we have a problem. More guns are not the answer. Arming teachers is not the answer. Single entry doors are not the answer. Armed guards at school is not the answer — and let’s be clear, there were police and security guards on site in Uvalde. Keeping guns out of the hands of teenagers and those who should not have them is the answer — we need red flag laws. Background checks are the answer. Waiting periods are the answer. Safe storage laws are the answer. Nearly 90% of Americans , regardless of party affiliation, think we should pass common sense gun laws, and yet Republicans in Congress and in state legislatures across the country continue to block these much-needed reforms. Why are the politicians we elect not doing what we, their bosses (their constituents) need them to do? The answer is pretty simple: money. They are more concerned about donations and holding on to power than doing the right thing. Texas’s Gov. Abbott went to a fundraiser the evening after the second largest mass shooting in his state and lawmakers are flocking to a NRA convention in Houston just days after 19 children and two teachers were murdered by guns 300 miles away. And, for those who say that this isn’t the time to politicize this, tell me, when is the right time? This is the 212 th mass shooting THIS YEAR! Enough is enough. We need to start holding those who block gun reform efforts accountable. One way we do this is to VOTE . I know this may not feel like it is enough, but it is a VERY powerful tool, one we each have and must use accordingly. We can also speak out bravely, like former Congressman Beto O’Rourke [] did when he told Gov. Abbott “This is on you!” at a press conference. To learn more about gun violence, sensible gun safety legislation, and what you can do to help, check out these resources: Laura Nevitt Operations Manager Keystone Progress [] Paid for by Keystone Progress Education Fund, www.kpef.info [] . Not affiliated with any candidate or candidate's committees. Keystone Progress 354 S Atlantic Ave Pittsburgh, PA 15224 If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: .

Peter,
---
This fight isn't over. While Biden's Orwellian "Disinformation Board" is shuttered, there are still _**MAJOR QUESTIONS**_ that must be answered. Under Joe Biden, the Department of Homeland Security has been anything but secure. Starting with the crisis at the border, and continuing through this dystopian disinformation board, DHS has been an _**UNMITIGATED DISASTER**_. I'm fighting back and demanding accountability for this recklessness. The citizens of Missouri and the rest of the country _**DESERVE ANSWERS**_. We will get to the source of this disastrous decision. If you want accountability and answers for the Biden Administration's disastrous decisions, donate today: [**https://secure.winred.com/josh-hawley-committee/dhs_transparency**](http://email.hawleyformo.com/u/click?_t=11a592fdc01846d4a1c728421295853d&_m=90820262a94b4bd3a7c30de641033c69&_e=gXqVrK-PQtrj5iwtwYQl-Mwe6NvQpgFT9J3YQ3nTj_bR69rMJSqmeaqHvnU1lvh6wTWfy121xVvzAuo1WQn08_TGXJAMYxvi32efOa_CH4PJ_aaCl95nBtcUCf8pcMPv5WOqLAQOvX-zvu3z3wcoEPfk5I_6ZoZEJDljgYu5qLdRVjGe4IpSPmLvc44Kwoy8uoyYeYw90CVK0zKyA8-OFd5bqQBhsZfge_kN97-jVvXQXvsfgbiaf2-JYOqIzvIpsbLhnjNtctZDD66ZrzzXYb4PdkuPgrohBMlVeq4l8QK37McfqbEs1pZseuK9w-iO3i4r_qIyyTFfmH43elNQKUB0L_5ApXaXHiO7Se-dVOChKx1R7a4Et8-KyFuBnL1lSNXnvp6KSi5T8l3Fxja6ef10igu9fVDT7CpgdfM9nZw%3D).

name: Our Revolution
subject: An insurrectionist as PA Governor? We’re taking action
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Our Revolution click here to unsubscribe
body:
[ stdin# ][IMG]

Peter Willis — we need you to help defend democracy from right-wing extremists!!

The Republicans nominated an insurrectionist Doug Mastriano for governor. He spread Trump’s Big Lie to undermine faith in democracy and even participated in the January 6th Capitol riot himself!

We can stop Mastriano before it’s too late. Our Revolution is calling on Secretary of the Commonwealth Leigh Chapman to bar insurrectionists — Doug Mastriano and Donald Trump - from the Pennsylvania ballot based on her power under the US Constitution.

In addition, Representative Scott Perry (PA-10) was involved in trying to overturn the results of the 2020 election and needs to be investigated as to whether he is disqualified, under Section 3 of the 14th Amendment, from holding future public office.

Peter Willis, sign here if you agree that Secretary Chapman must take action to bar insurrectionists from the ballot for violating our Constitution! (https://act.ourrevolution.com/signup/143_PA_PT/?t=1011&akid=2964%2E5885482%2Ejg0572)

Section 3 of the 14th Amendment, also known as the Insurrectionist Disqualification Clause, says that anyone who has taken an oath to uphold The Constitution and then turns around and engages in insurrection against the United States isn’t fit for office.

That’s why Our Revolution is working with lawyers at Free Speech for People to keep oath-breaking extremists from ever serving in government again.

And, we need progressive patriots to stand up with us in the fight — Sign our petition! (https://act.ourrevolution.com/signup/143_PA_PT/?t=1012&akid=2964%2E5885482%2Ejg0572)

DEFEND DEMOCRACY (https://act.ourrevolution.com/signup/143_PA_PT/?t=1013&akid=2964%2E5885482%2Ejg0572)

In solidarity,

Our Revolution

name: Dan Crenshaw
subject: Everything is on the line
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hunt for Congress
body:
Wesley’s congressional race will decide the fate of the House majority.
| Friend,
---
Wesley told me you were one of his most trusted supporters, so I wanted to personally reach out with the end-of-month deadline just days away.
---
In Florida alone, Governor DeSantis’ new congressional map gives us 4 more Republican-leaning districts.
---
Which means we can END Pelosi’s majority once and for all right here in the Lone Star State by winning Texas’ brand new 38th district – _the FINAL SEAT needed to flip the House_.

**_There’s no way around it, everything is on the line in Wesley’s race. But as it stands, he’s still $38,310 short of his April fundraising goal._**

Which means we can END Pelosi’s majority once and for all right here in the Lone Star State by winning Texas’ brand new 38th district – the FINAL SEAT needed to flip the House. There’s no way around it, everything is on the line in Wesley’s race. But as it stands, he’s still $38,310 short of his April fundraising goal. The Democrats are facing a free fall of epic proportions, Friend. body:
I’m ringing the alarm...
Peter,

We can’t take anything for granted, especially after national Republicans poured $1.4 million in dark money into our district in order to drag Edwards across the finish line in the primary. We can bet they’ll spend even more as this race continues to tighten.

**In 2021, the HIGHEST NUMBER of illegal immigrants came to our Southern border in 20 years.**

Our brave Department of Homeland Security Officers are trying their best, but it’s _impossible_ for anyone to keep up with the insane number of illegal criminals trying to cross into our country.
They need our help - but Joe Biden and the Democrats are treating them as criminals instead of brave law enforcement officers trying to keep America safe.
If we’re going to give our DHS officers the backup we need, we MUST build President Trump’s border wall.

Hello,
This week, Russia banned 963 Americans from entering the country. The list included President Biden, Vice President Harris, Secretary Clinton, Speaker Pelosi, and even Republicans like Kevin McCarthy and Marco Rubio.
Notably missing: Donald Trump, Mitch McConnell, and my opponent Chuck Grassley. Why would war criminal Vladimir Putin be okay with those three? Why the red carpet for those comrades?
Chuck Grassley was formally endorsed and embraced by Putin’s greatest ally, Donald Trump, and his silence has been golden. He was complicit in failing to properly investigate Russia’s involvement in the 2016 election. And Grassley has fueled Russian disinformation theories about Ukraine over and over again.
By not refuting lies while actively spreading disinformation, Grassley has become central to the dysfunction and dissension in America. His vote against a bipartisan commission to investigate the January 6th insurrection will forever stain his legacy.
The fate and future of our democracy are in grave danger, and I know I will be on the right side of history. Someone should ask Senator Grassley, “Whose side are you on?”
I served our country in the United States Navy for almost 4 decades. When I defeat Chuck Grassley, I will be the most senior military person ever-elected to the U.S. Senate. You can be sure I’ll earn my ban from Russia by standing up for democracy here at home and abroad.
**But Chuck Grassley just launched his first massive TV ad buy attacking me, and we need to raise $50,000 to respond and fight back. I’m not taking a dime of corporate PAC money, so if I’m going to win and send Grassley packing, I need grassroots donors like you to give now:**
[**Please, may I count on you to give $5 or more right now – every dollar you can afford – to help me defeat Chuck Grassley, turn Iowa blue, and protect our Democratic majority in the Senate?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/45780620/347459629/-42398318?refcode=em20220526pm2-nd&amounts=5%2C10%2C25%2C35%2C50%2C100%2C250&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85NDI4MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjZjE2MmUxMC01MmRkLWVjMTEtYjY1Ni0yODE4NzhiODUxMTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=VUlOLgjGBH8TniZflopAsCjabBwKFgva3ZBesBkaygM=)

clean_body: Hello, This week, Russia banned 963 Americans from entering the country. The list included President Biden, Vice President Harris, Secretary Clinton, Speaker Pelosi, and even Republicans like Kevin McCarthy and Marco Rubio. Notably missing: Donald Trump, Mitch McConnell, and my opponent Chuck Grassley. Why would war criminal Vladimir Putin be okay with those three? Why the red carpet for those comrades? Chuck Grassley was formally endorsed and embraced by Putin’s greatest ally, Donald Trump, and his silence has been golden. He was complicit in failing to properly investigate Russia’s involvement in the 2016 election. And Grassley has fueled Russian disinformation theories about Ukraine over and over again. By not refuting lies while actively spreading disinformation, Grassley has become central to the dysfunction and dissension in America. His vote against a bipartisan commission to investigate the January 6th insurrection will forever stain his legacy. The fate and future of our democracy are in grave danger, and I know I will be on the right side of history. Someone should ask Senator Grassley, “Whose side are you on?” I served our country in the United States Navy for almost 4 decades. When I defeat Chuck Grassley, I will be the most senior military person ever-elected to the U.S. Senate. You can be sure I’ll earn my ban from Russia by standing up for democracy here at home and abroad. But Chuck Grassley just launched his first massive TV ad buy attacking me, and we need to raise $50,000 to respond and fight back. I’m not taking a dime of corporate PAC money, so if I’m going to win and send Grassley packing, I need grassroots donors like you to give now: = If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | = | = | | = | = | | = | = There’s only one way to take on all of Grassley’s special interest money, and it’s with grassroots support from people like you now. Thank you for everything you do, Mike Franken, U.S. Navy (ret.) Paid for by Michael Franken for Iowa, Inc Michael Franken for Iowa, Inc PO Box 13401 Des Moines, IA 50310 United States

body:
| This is the toughest race Dan has ever faced, and his opponents and out-of-state, dark-money Super PACs have reserved _hundreds of thousands of dollars_ to flood the airwaves in mid-Michigan with ads trying to defeat us — **and one of our opponents just dropped his first negative ad against Dan this week.**

Make no mistake: we have to keep up our work building a winning campaign if we are going to be successful.

**This month, we’re hiring our first field organizers to be the voice of our campaign in the district and we’re getting ready to do even more grassroots organizing for the summer. From phone banking to canvassing — it’s reaching voters directly and combating mistruths from our opponents with neighbor-to-neighbor conversations that will make the difference in November.**

**[Can we count on you to step up and contribute $15 or any amount right now to help fund our organizing and outreach efforts? I joined Team Kildee because I wanted to work with a proven champion for working families in Michigan. Dan has fought to lower the price of things like gasoline and prescription drugs, and is committed to building things in Michigan, not China.

Dan is a proven leader who gets things done and delivers for mid-Michigan. That’s why I’m proud to be on Team Kildee, and why I hope you’ll join us.

clean_body: | This is the toughest race Dan has ever faced, and his opponents and out-of-state, dark-money Super PACs have reserved hundreds of thousands of dollars to flood the airwaves in mid-Michigan with ads trying to defeat us — and one of our opponents just dropped his first negative ad against Dan this week. Make no mistake: we have to keep up our work building a winning campaign if we are going to be successful. This month, we’re hiring our first field organizers to be the voice of our campaign in the district and we’re getting ready to do even more grassroots organizing for the summer. From phone banking to canvassing — it’s reaching voters directly and combating mistruths from our opponents with neighbor-to-neighbor conversations that will make the difference in November. = If you saved your payment information with ActBlue Express clicking a button below will cause your donation to process instantly | | = | = | = | = | = | = I joined Team Kildee because I wanted to work with a proven champion for working families in Michigan. Dan has fought to lower the price of things like gasoline and prescription drugs, and is committed to building things in Michigan, not China. Dan is a proven leader who gets things done and delivers for mid-Michigan. That’s why I’m proud to be on Team Kildee, and why I hope you’ll join us. = Thanks, Danya Rafiqi Campaign Manager, Friends of Dan Kildee Congressman Dan Kildee – a Flint native – is proud to represent mid-Michigan. As a Democratic party leader, Dan is fighting hard to ensure every American has access to quality health care, clean water and a good-paying job. The path to protect the Democratic majority in the House runs right through the Midwest, and Dan is dedicated to protecting and electing Democratic candidates. But we can’t do it without you! = | = | = Paid for by Friends of Dan Kildee Friends of Dan Kildee P.O. Box 248 Flint, MI 48501 United States **

name: Marisa Wood
subject: My heart is still broken
body:
|
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/45780699/347463023/883924314?refcode=em-header&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDU3NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMGI4ODk3OC01M2RkLWVjMTEtYjY1Ni0yODE4NzhiODUxMTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=rPoqtUFK5a2xOemWdUU9xPXmO37a2QBBmbY775cDwsw=)
Friend,

It's been two days since the horrific shooting at Robb Elementary school in Uvlade, Texas. But my heart is still broken thinking about those children and the tragic loss of life.

As a public school teacher, it's impossible to see those pictures from Uvalde and not think of the students I serve here in Bakersfield. It's a tragedy that today's students have to worry about being "next" when they arrive at school in the morning. This was certainly not the case for my generation.

We cannot turn a blind eye to these heinous mass shootings. Inaction is not an option. Because if history is any indicator, the clock is ticking until the next massacre. We are losing time.

We must legislate to protect innocent lives. We can enforce stricter gun registration rules, stronger background checks, banning of assault rifles, and crackdown on the sale of ghost guns. We must also focus on the cause of these shootings and ensure comprehensive mental health awareness. It also means holding social media platforms accountable for the spread of hatred.

I'm frustrated, friend. And I'm sure you are, too. But our shared anger and unfathomable sadness will mean nothing if we don’t make our voices heard at the ballot box. The June 7 primaries are around the corner. We can make a change by voting out all the politicians who refuse to stand up to the NRA. The time is now!

Thanks for your support,

Marisa Wood
Public School Teacher for Congress
---

As a public school teacher, it's impossible to see those pictures from Uvalde and not think of the students I serve here in Bakersfield. It's a tragedy that today's students have to worry about being "next" when they arrive at school in the morning. This was certainly not the case for my generation. We cannot turn a blind eye to these heinous mass shootings. Inaction is not an option. Because if history is any indicator, the clock is ticking until the next massacre. We are losing time. We must legislate to protect innocent lives. We can enforce stricter gun registration rules, stronger background checks, banning of assault rifles, and crackdown on the sale of ghost guns. We must also focus on the cause of these shootings and ensure comprehensive mental health awareness. It also means holding social media platforms accountable for the spread of hatred. I'm frustrated, friend. And I'm sure you are, too. But our shared anger and unfathomable sadness will mean nothing if we don’t make our voices heard at the ballot box. The June 7 primaries are around the corner. We can make a change by voting out all the politicians who refuse to stand up to the NRA. The time is now! Thanks for your support, Marisa Wood Public School Teacher for Congress | = Marisa Wood is a public school teacher and Democrat running a historic campaign to unseat GOP Leader Kevin McCarthy in California's 23rd District. 2022 is our best shot in decades to finally defeat McCarthy. Consider supporting Marisa's campaign by = If you'd prefer to send a check, our address is: Marisa Wood for Congress, PO Box 12103, Bakersfield, CA 93389. We'd hate to see you go, but you can click here if you'd like to . Marisa Wood for Congress PO Box 12103 Bakersfield, CA 93389 United States

#
Hey there,
We’re writing to ask you to make a donation -- of $20 or whatever you can afford to give -- to Alex Padilla’s campaign for U.S. Senate. Here’s why we’re asking:
* We’re just a few weeks away from our primary election, and now is about the time our campaign is making important strategic decisions about how much and where to advertise, our GOTV operation, and more.

* But the truth is our fundraising this month has been slower than anticipated, and right now we’re not sure if we’re going to be able to hit our May goal.

* There’s a possibility that after the primary, our opponent will be a right-wing Republican who’s argued against mask mandates and against the right to choose. And he is going to spend millions of dollars attacking Alex. The stakes are too high to risk being caught unprepared.

name: Val Demings
subject: End the filibuster and pass gun reform legislation to protect our children.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Val Demings for U
body:
This makes me sick, friend
| | |
---
This makes me sick, friend: It's been two days since a mass shooting at an elementary school left 19 families each mourning the loss of a child – _**and the GOP is likely already planning to block any legislation that would provide meaningful change.**_
It's not enough to just offer these grieving families our _"thoughts and prayers."_ I grew up in the church, and let me tell you this: Faith without works is _dead._ It's time for Congress to take immediate action and save lives from gun violence.
**There's one major obstacle in our way: The GOP has used the filibuster to block our Democratic agenda in the Senate for years – and there’s no doubt in my mind that Republicans are willing to use it again to stop our gun safety reform bill from becoming law.**
[**That's why I'm calling on you to take immediate action because we can’t afford to wait another day: Will you sign my petition to abolish the filibuster so that Senate Democrats can pass our bill and protect our**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/45780827/347465524/1213799187?code=jtk3345-su-nd&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84Mzc4NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzY2I4MzMyNi01NGRkLWVjMTEtYjY1Ni0yODE4NzhiODUxMTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=NRvvvalnc7YuHo8CHrmiLvH9keawbLvhf6twXsN7of8=&emci=6021893e-11dd-ec11-b656-281878b8c32f&emdi=3cb83326-54dd-ec11-b656-281878b85110&ceid=20244100)**[communities from gun violence?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/45780828/347465525/1213799187?code=jtk3345-su-nd&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84Mzc4NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzY2I4MzMyNi01NGRkLWVjMTEtYjY1Ni0yODE4NzhiODUxMTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=NRvvvalnc7YuHo8CHrmiLvH9keawbLvhf6twXsN7of8=&emci=6021893e-11dd-ec11-b656-281878b8c32f&emdi=3cb83326-54dd-ec11-b656-281878b85110&ceid=20244100)**
| | [ADD MY NAME](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/45780829/347465526/1213799187?code=jtk3345-su-nd&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84Mzc4NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzY2I4MzMyNi01NGRkLWVjMTEtYjY1Ni0yODE4NzhiODUxMTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=NRvvvalnc7YuHo8CHrmiLvH9keawbLvhf6twXsN7of8=&emci=6021893e-11dd-ec11-b656-281878b8c32f&emdi=3cb83326-54dd-ec11-b656-281878b85110&ceid=20244100)

name: Derek Marshall
subject: We can't go on like this
body:
| | |
---
I’ve got two 2-year-old nephews. I don’t want them growing up in a country where week-in and week-out, we have nonstop gun violence. **I’m running for Congress because we can’t go on like this. We just can’t.** We need universal background checks, red flag laws, and an end to the gun show loopholes – all popular, commonsense measures. Instead, what we hear from Congress is excuses and delays. Congress is going on a holiday recess while the NRA is pressing ahead with its huge annual conference. My heart breaks for the families of the children of Uvalde, and for our society that struggles to overcome the violence. We need to elect more members of Congress who support common sense gun laws, and aren’t beholden to the gun lobby. We need to do what it takes to protect our children. In these last few weeks before the June 7 California primary, I’ll be thinking of my nephews as we work to get out the vote. I want them to have the freedom to go to school and live their lives without fear of mass violence. **I haven’t forgotten that a better world is possible. Together, let’s make it happen.** In solidarity, – Derek

One of the most important programs we run at VoteVets is one that connects veterans to veterans directly via phone and text message.
#
Derek —
One of the most important programs we run at VoteVets is one that connects veterans to veterans directly via phone and text message.
Our VoteVets veteran volunteers talk to other veterans about disinformation. They talk about important races and issues.
And most importantly, they talk about getting out to vote.
For far too long, the word “veteran” was taken as synonymous with “conservative.”
That’s not an accident. That’s because conservatives prioritized organizing veterans. And it worked: Remember the “Swift Boat Veterans for Truth?”
Those days are over thanks to VoteVets.
But we cannot run this program alone:

name: Karen Bass
subject: I will be talking about Uvalde, Texas in this email.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Karen Bass for Mayor 2022 (ID # 1441328)
body:
Please only read if you feel comfortable doing so.

My heart is breaking, friend.

I am sending my thoughts to the victims, the survivors, their loved ones, and the entire Uvalde community. My heart breaks for the lives shattered by this senseless act of violence. These were babies. You can't quantify this kind of loss. It is too big, too inhumane, and too cruel to comprehend.

This level of carnage should not be normal. But in America, our children practice active shooter drills. Our seniors fear a trip to the grocery store. And parents worry that a teenager's evening stroll may be their last. It shouldn't be normal to feel unsafe at school, church, the grocery store, the park, and in our own skins.

We can't ignore that this doesn't happen anywhere else. We can't ignore that so many mass shootings are racially-motivated. And we can't ignore that lawmakers – my colleagues – would rather blame individuals instead of standing up to the gun lobby.

I've been in this fight for a long time, and I'm not giving up now.

As a Moms Demand Action Gun Sense Candidate, I will continue to fight for gun safety in Los Angeles. I will fight to protect our kids and our communities. We must respond emphatically, forcefully, and immediately to keep our neighborhoods safe.

In Congress, I helped pass legislation that will expand background checks for gun sales and close the 'Charleston loophole'. I'm with you all in demanding the Senate pass gun safety legislation.

Today, I am thinking of the brave words of youth activist Edna Chavez from Community Coalition, who said:

**"I have lived in South L.A. my entire life and have lost many loved ones to gun violence. This is normal, normal to the point that I have learned to duck from bullets before I learned how to read."**

No more. Not in Charleston, not in Uvalde, and certainly not in Los Angeles. As mayor, I will use every tool at my disposal to keep our city safe from gun violence. I am angry. I am standing with you in grief. And I will keep fighting.

Sincerely,

Karen

Paid for by Karen Bass for Mayor 2022 (ID # 1441328)

Paid for by Karen Bass for Mayor 2022
777 S Figueroa St
STE 4050

clean_body: Please only read if you feel comfortable doing so. My heart is breaking, friend. I am sending my thoughts to the victims, the survivors, their loved ones, and the entire Uvalde community. My heart breaks for the lives shattered by this senseless act of violence. These were babies. You can't quantify this kind of loss. It is too big, too inhumane, and too cruel to comprehend. This level of carnage should not be normal. But in America, our children practice active shooter drills. Our seniors fear a trip to the grocery store. And parents worry that a teenager's evening stroll may be their last. It shouldn't be normal to feel unsafe at school, church, the grocery store, the park, and in our own skins. We can't ignore that this doesn't happen anywhere else. We can't ignore that so many mass shootings are racially-motivated. And we can't ignore that lawmakers – my colleagues – would rather blame individuals instead of standing up to the gun lobby. I've been in this fight for a long time, and I'm not giving up now. As a Moms Demand Action Gun Sense Candidate, I will continue to fight for gun safety in Los Angeles. I will fight to protect our kids and our communities. We must respond emphatically, forcefully, and immediately to keep our neighborhoods safe. In Congress, I helped pass legislation that will expand background checks for gun sales and close the 'Charleston loophole'. I'm with you all in demanding the Senate pass gun safety legislation. Today, I am thinking of the brave words of youth activist Edna Chavez from Community Coalition, who said: "I have lived in South L.A. my entire life and have lost many loved ones to gun violence. This is normal, normal to the point that I have learned to duck from bullets before I learned how to read." No more. Not in Charleston, not in Uvalde, and certainly not in Los Angeles. As mayor, I will use every tool at my disposal to keep our city safe from gun violence. I am angry. I am standing with you in grief. And I will keep fighting. Sincerely, Karen Paid for by Karen Bass for Mayor 2022 (ID # 1441328) Paid for by Karen Bass for Mayor 2022 777 S Figueroa St STE 4050 Los Angeles, CA 90017 United States Additional information is available at . | | ---|---|--- We rely on this email to communicate with this grassroots team, but if you no longer want to receive our emails, please .

| Team, my name is Kris Mayes, and I am the Democratic nominee for Attorney General in Arizona.

My campaign has always been an important one, because here in Arizona, our current Attorney General is giving into election conspiracy nonsense and so is every single Republican trying to replace him.

But earlier this month, my campaign became even more crucial when we found out that the Supreme Court intends to overturn Roe v. Wade and send reproductive rights decisions back to the states. We know this will be terrible for Arizonans, and all six of my potential opponents are desperately trying to outdo each other by coming up with ways to restrict reproductive rights even more.

So let me be clear – **_Arizona will never prosecute anyone seeking an abortion or any licensed medical doctors or anyone providing licensed medical care, including abortion care, if I’m elected as Attorney General, period._**

**[Team, it’s clear the fight for health care rights is coming to the states, making positions like Attorney General very, very important. Will you split a donation of $10 or more between my campaign and the Arizona Democratic Party to make sure Arizonans don’t live in fear of prosecution for their health care decisions?](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953y_lA8GMASvCbIPPyR9ypl_JoJKkKX7dum5dTV0SQ0XcmuWR7Uu74EI4LkmETzzIZuWTJ_P0PZiGDPTlS0RvP9z3SQFSTFWJYbufno23QSpYpn7tyochfhkMr5cw_GO9DyT-1hExOHuCnzovOwEQsNtQX6KCLpXLwJgR4LBTWdTkvS20itmTRBoR820PP3LVKMQjARXVKxw3xnSSFV1dDtIvmFH6deeM82EU1adqFrUbSKkyXSj0UletIQKm27YUB1GOQXSjYV_7BtisbR6GLayDqKUXm6ePa-B7TS3wuID6pHz8xN92DRgqXDAr2FvLx4w_aM-kHojwIplxmhcp1-iEyJsOXBAOjHM2mJKJVk7jn89t2Fc9uA3_71T51HEbL_l9TR65sgvc24iBexTjY1e7FaTG-qOy2PXsZ_h_jxhxajJUfTTmrbi8FB3xmBzL4Q/3md/7EjpNks8Ss2_CXU_7OItGw/h1/P_xpQZOBsIDLl-dwEbJSf5JpcXYj0ZK3l0wRz0VEvIU)** [
DONATE ](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953y_lA8GMASvCbIPPyR9ypl_JoJKkKX7dum5dTV0SQ0XcmuWR7Uu74EI4LkmETzzIZuWTJ_P0PZiGDPTlS0RvP9zQ3SXUITH54JFWE9zLgRPkB6aCZ3IUCk6osRV-tOPLQCdBYT1EImJZG3peNgs9P26dZ4IAooAc4oHDQabiSMQC--YoMeKBm23SKWIuyWS434QVFTpoynCtvrLYMqhFUYYXQrNlYODKjeTFis6vqE92MRKi4r5vNTsBoA-em3KWW_KkJXm85fJhqYZupFtZkADXWlHAovzUb6N6Avar1smuAMqMIfZg86qq_iyhdYoZx6SFOjSKmA0CzQWqofLb8sDTZU1CYYqyYSFNSZjgIb78--XpW_oh5FYl4tnc9QVHxSbJ_ga3nM7eg0U-WZBIqIMofIhKp_HqC_BeNs3taUUIlSvz1kBJ-3Yh3gOPHtYJjo/3md/7EjpNks8Ss2_CXU_7OItGw/h2/tmb_khyvGM_8QF4pvZ82HOtvZhihM2Qc1MN5MgUqvbY)
Republicans have already put Arizonans’ health decisions in peril by passing a 15-week abortion ban, with no exceptions for rape or incest, modeled after the Mississippi law, earlier this year. An even older law banning abortions could go into effect once the Supreme Court’s decision is final.

If given the opportunity, I have no doubt the AZ GOP will target doctors and their patients for prosecution. We’ve already seen what this looks like in Texas, and we don’t need that here in Arizona.

Medical decisions are between doctors and their patients. As Arizona Attorney General, I’ll focus on real issues and stay out of doctor’s offices.

**[Team, will you split a donation of $10 or more between AZ Dems and my campaign to protect our rights? We can’t afford to lose this office and put Arizonans at risk of prosecution for medical decisions.](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953y_lA8GMASvCbIPPyR9ypl_JoJKkKX7dum5dTV0SQ0XcmuWR7Uu74EI4LkmETzzIZuWTJ_P0PZiGDPTlS0RvP9zOEjeEgylH-eaS0OKry61C-ipdVNCwAq1dfizHdhDuD3A5xCOuP9EZGS4lRVEYZlKr6qH-pXJLwuY9I55w_LnyVVeWjQCqCgobWK6EDorKpaIGCA9vRFzAouQoM4lyUg12Kzr2caqS54A7WZWXlX_Oq4h7xpoDhgofovY3-o7BwnHJ0EItQ0fjA3bB2TOuc9rEgzbtOa8xYFJbrq8FjVxlcFpljFo5zuCiETKWYHilUrESapRr1h4495-tDJRhzu1FcP0pFqnghqoziWVuA5evdoqgGlxMDLaN5k06lOsarB7RBAllRBuvKy3M-gf-Lc3AomQMzaZPhoDv9uZeF56DysDDCy2KsEzMboEWznsEQU/3md/7EjpNks8Ss2_CXU_7OItGw/h3/DjBXSeLIJUM4sxKdbksuHoc1CTp4eKJaA57Zf9Uc0yo)**

clean_body: | | | I need your support | | | Team, my name is Kris Mayes, and I am the Democratic nominee for Attorney General in Arizona. My campaign has always been an important one, because here in Arizona, our current Attorney General is giving into election conspiracy nonsense and so is every single Republican trying to replace him. But earlier this month, my campaign became even more crucial when we found out that the Supreme Court intends to overturn Roe v. Wade and send reproductive rights decisions back to the states. We know this will be terrible for Arizonans, and all six of my potential opponents are desperately trying to outdo each other by coming up with ways to restrict reproductive rights even more. So let me be clear – Arizona will never prosecute anyone seeking an abortion or any licensed medical doctors or anyone providing licensed medical care, including abortion care, if I’m elected as Attorney General, period. Republicans have already put Arizonans’ health decisions in peril by passing a 15-week abortion ban, with no exceptions for rape or incest, modeled after the Mississippi law, earlier this year. An even older law banning abortions could go into effect once the Supreme Court’s decision is final. If given the opportunity, I have no doubt the AZ GOP will target doctors and their patients for prosecution. We’ve already seen what this looks like in Texas, and we don’t need that here in Arizona. Medical decisions are between doctors and their patients. As Arizona Attorney General, I’ll focus on real issues and stay out of doctor’s offices. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation to Kris Mayes and the Arizona Democratic Party will go through immediately: Thank you for your support, Kris For up-to-date COVID-19 information and guidance, visit Paid for by the Arizona Democratic Party and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. Rick McGuire Treasurer. Arizona Democratic Party 2910 North Central Ave Phoenix AZ 85012 United States | | Sent via . name: Team Sarah
subject: Our momentum is surging
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Sarah for Wisconsin
body:
It’s important that you know three things about the current state of our U.S. Senate race in Wisconsin. Please keep reading.
1. **Our momentum is surging** after we released three TV ads to compete with Ron Johnson on the airwaves, get Sarah's message out to Wisconsin voters, and grow our polling lead against him.

2. The campaign was featured on MSNBC and CNN, over 100 people came to a recent grassroots event, and this is our best fundraising month yet.

3. But National Republicans just reserved $9 million on TV to protect Ron Johnson — that’s on top of the $15 million Mitch McConnell’s Super PAC has already booked in this race.

Look, Wisconsin is now at the top of the GOP’s defensive priorities because they’re getting nervous about us and they want to blunt our campaign’s momentum.

Republicans only need to flip ONE seat to take back the Senate — and they can’t afford to lose Ron Johnson. So they’re going to spend whatever it takes to try to sink us, and we need your help to keep our TV ads running.

**[We are not accepting any corporate PAC money. Instead, we are relying on your support. Anything you can chip in before our end-of-month deadline will allow us to compete with Ron Johnson and the Republicans on TV so we can flip this seat and expand our Senate majority.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/45775802/347433989/43152360?refcode=em22_220526_EOM1_fnd_gq_act___ts_ettx&utm_campaign=em22_220526_EOM1_fnd_gq_act___ts_ettx&utm_medium=email&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODI4NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4MDVhZTliMC0zN2RkLWVjMTEtYjY1Ni0yODE4NzhiODUxMTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ifEkWNIghuNvn298dXP1jPzODeZTrVgCzyXDqqHTgOU=)** | [Contribute](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/45775803/347433994/43152360?refcode=em22_220526_EOM1_fnd_gq_act___ts_ettx&utm_campaign=em22_220526_EOM1_fnd_gq_act___ts_ettx&utm_medium=email&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODI4NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4MDVhZTliMC0zN2RkLWVjMTEtYjY1Ni0yODE4NzhiODUxMTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ifEkWNIghuNvn298dXP1jPzODeZTrVgCzyXDqqHTgOU=)

The momentum we’re seeing in the polls, the press, and the energy on the ground is incredible. We have a real shot at defeating Ron Johnson and flipping this Senate seat from red to blue, but we need as many resources as possible to keep our campaign growing in the critical months ahead.

That’s why your contribution is so important today. Thanks for reading, and thanks for all you do to support our campaign.

— Team Sarah
Sarah Godlewski is running for U.S. Senate to put Wisconsin workers and families first. She's the only Democrat who is beating Ron Johnson in the latest head-to-head poll of our race. clean_body: It’s important that you know three things about the current state of our U.S. Senate race in Wisconsin. Please keep reading. 1. Our momentum is surging after we released three TV ads to compete with Ron Johnson on the airwaves, get Sarah's message out to Wisconsin voters, and grow our polling lead against him. 2. The campaign was featured on MSNBC and CNN, over 100 people came to a recent grassroots event, and this is our best fundraising month yet. 3. But National Republicans just reserved $9 million on TV to protect Ron Johnson — that’s on top of the $15 million Mitch McConnell’s Super PAC has already booked in this race. Look, Wisconsin is now at the top of the GOP’s defensive priorities because they’re getting nervous about us and they want to blunt our campaign’s momentum. Republicans only need to flip ONE seat to take back the Senate — and they can’t afford to lose Ron Johnson. So they’re going to spend whatever it takes to try to sink us, and we need your help to keep our TV ads running. = | = The momentum we’re seeing in the polls, the press, and the energy on the ground is incredible. We have a real shot at defeating Ron Johnson and flipping this Senate seat from red to blue, but we need as many resources as possible to keep our campaign growing in the critical months ahead. That’s why your contribution is so important today. Thanks for reading, and thanks for all you do to support our campaign. — Team Sarah Sarah Godlewski is running for U.S. Senate to put Wisconsin workers and families first. She's the only Democrat who is beating Ron Johnson in the latest head-to-head poll of our race. With your help, we can flip this seat. Chip in to join us: | = Paid for by Sarah for Wisconsin
